column1: # Human-skim decisions applied on top of the seed_language.py heuristic.
# Columns: path (relative to OpenData) <TAB> 1-based CSV row <TAB> value <TAB> note
# An empty value suppresses a false positive; a value forces that language.
# Ann Kneale documents contain blocks where the columns are swapped (the Manx
# column holds English verse and vice versa). The heuristic misses the short
# lines; these rows complete those blocks.

column1: # Code-switching rows which are genuinely half Manx, half English.
# In the UOSH transcripts, 'en' is only for entirely English lines: a line
# containing any Manx utterance (even one word, or a cited Manx form) is 'mixed'.
# Manx place names used within an English sentence do not count as Manx.

column4: And your cellar lane lhune mie.
